headline: Fintech dLocal posts first-quarter profit down 10%
summary: Payments provider dLocal on Thursday reported a 10% dip in ‌its first-quarter net profit missing ‌analyst estimates as a one-off tax charge and ​higher expenses offset a surge in payment volumes.  Net profit for the period hit $41.9 million, falling short of the $48.9 million ‌expected by ⁠analysts.  Total payment volume (TPV) surged 73% to $14.1 billion, up from $8.1 billion ‌a year earlier.

raw.headline: Fintech dLocal posts first-quarter profit down 10%
raw.summary: Payments provider dLocal on Thursday reported a 10% dip in ‌its first-quarter net profit missing ‌analyst estimates as a one-off tax charge and ​higher expenses offset a surge in payment volumes.  Net profit for the period hit $41.9 million, falling short of the $48.9 million ‌expected by ⁠analysts.  Total payment volume (TPV) surged 73% to $14.1 billion, up from $8.1 billion ‌a year earlier.

headline: How The Uber Technologies (UBER) Narrative Is Shifting Around Autonomy Deals And Valuation
summary: Uber Technologies is back in focus after an updated analyst fair value estimate moved from US$103.46 to US$105.01, a modest shift that still reflects a careful reassessment of the stock. Behind this adjustment is a mix of optimism and caution around automation plans with partners like Nvidia and Zoox, the integration of deals such as Blacklane, and how management handles capital returns. Read on to see what is driving this evolving narrative and how you can track it over time. Stay updated as...

headline: Fleet Launches Mythos-ready Autonomous Endpoint Management
summary: A vulnerability discovered on Friday can be weaponized by Monday, yet the average enterprise patch cycle still takes 55 to 94 days. To address this challenge, Fleet today announced autonomous endpoint management that cuts that window to under two weeks - and in many cases, hours - through continuous patching and down-to-the-hour vulnerability exposure reporting across every major operating system.

raw.headline: Fleet Launches Mythos-ready Autonomous Endpoint Management

headline: Uber Reshapes Leadership To Tackle AI And Autonomous Vehicle Risks
summary: Uber Technologies (NYSE:UBER) has appointed Jill Hazelbaker as President and Chief Corporate Affairs Officer. The new role consolidates leadership of people, corporate affairs, and safety operations under one executive. The transition includes the departure of Chief People Officer Nikki Krishnamurthy as Uber invests heavily in AI and autonomous vehicles. Uber enters this leadership transition with its stock trading at $74.7 and a mixed performance profile. The share price is up 97.4% over...
